text: Handling deadlines is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. Effective time management starts with breaking large tasks into smaller, manageable steps and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization helps you focus on the most urgent or important tasks first, so you avoid wasting time on trivial activities. Scope management means sticking to the original plan and not adding extra work unless absolutely necessary. Finally, clear communication is key. If you anticipate a delay, inform your team or supervisor early and suggest a realistic solution. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress and better results.
